name: perf001_rolling_vol_vectorization_curated_inputs
description: Curated input set for PERF-001 rolling-vol vectorization. Drives both the numerical-identity tests (vectorized == Python-loop reference) and the run_monte_carlo end-to-end identity test (same seed, same input, same output). NO producer-computed values are pinned — every assertion compares the production call against an in-test reference implementation.
provenance: Schema-derived. The input shape (history kind, day count, amplitudes) is authored to exercise rolling-vol edge cases; outputs are NOT included. Day counts are derived from MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS + (MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1) at test time — never hardcoded.
pa18_note: No mc_prob, no spy_vol values are pinned. Tests assert vectorized_output == python_loop_reference_output, where the reference is computed in the test from the SAME inputs. The reference loop is the spec; the fixture supplies only inputs.

scenarios.description: Long history (minimum + 50 days). Alternating signs exercise the running-std window over a stationary regime.
Exactly minimum_raw_days. Pins that the window boundary at i==MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS-1 produces an identical std under vectorization.
minimum_raw_days + 1. Pins the off-by-one slice that a careless pandas rewrite would flip.
Monotonically-increasing returns. Rolling std on a monotone series is non-zero and changes day-to-day; tests catch a pandas implementation that drops bias-correction (ddof) silently.
Constant returns. Rolling std must be zero for every window after day 0. Pins that the vectorized path produces 0.0 (not a numerical-noise epsilon) on a degenerate window.
Wide-amplitude alternation. Stress-tests numerical precision on a high-vol regime.

derivation: raw_days = MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS + (MC_VOL_WINDOW_DAYS - 1) + raw_day_offset_from_minimum, read from math_engine at test time. last_percent_change=0.0 and spy_today_return=0.0 mirror the existing mc_rng_seeding fixture so the kNN match is mid-distribution (no saturation to 0/100).
